channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed

item.title: BioShock Creator: Switch 2 Proves Graphics Have Reached "Diminishing Returns"
item.content:encoded:
"People are realising"

When Switch 2 was revealed, the power under the hood of Nintendo's new hybrid device surprised many fans and developers. CD Projekt Red even displayed the system's powerful visual capabilities early on with Cyberpunk 2077.

Despite this, you're still likely to hear discussions about how long Nintendo's new device will be able to keep up with future releases and the rest of the market. While this is a valid concern, BioShock creator Ken Levine believes hardware such as the Switch 2 (and even Valve's upcoming Steam Machine) signal a shift.

Here's what he had to say to IGN recently about this (via Frvr):

"[Realism] doesn’t age as well as more stylistic things. BioShock still looks good, I think, because it wasn’t trying to get every nut and bolt super realistically rendered...it was more stylised.

“Look at say, the Switch 2 and [even] the new Steam Machine coming out. Those are not massive technological upgrades obviously. That wasn’t their strategy. I think, people are realising that we’ve sort of hit a bit of diminishing returns with that."

Levine says his own company, which is currently working on a spiritual successor to BioShock known as Judas, has never really focused on "ultra realism" because it's expensive, and as noted above, "doesn't age as well".

He sums it up, stating developers who have the "right art director" and the "right approach" don't need to be on the "cutting edge of technology all the time".

What are your thoughts about Levine's comments? Are ultra-realistic graphics your kind of thing, or would you rather see visuals that age more gracefully. Tell us in the comments.

[source ign.com, via frvr.com, mynintendonews.com]

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply


https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/05/bioshock-creator-switch-2-proves-graphics-have-reached-diminishing-returns
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Intel Rolls Out Arc Driver 8801, Adding Support For Forza Horizon 6 To Intel Arc A And B Series GPUs
item.content:encoded:
The latest Arc graphics driver is out, featuring support for new titles and fixes for various bugs on Intel Arc GPUs.

Intel Releases Arc Graphics Driver 32.0.101.8801 for Arc B and A Series GPUs, and Core Ultra With Arc iGPUs

Intel has officially rolled out its latest Arc Graphics Driver 32.0.101.8801, which adds support for new titles and fixes many bugs. In the release notes, Intel confirmed adding new games such as Forza Horizon 6, LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight, and Subnautica 2 for Intel Arc A and B series GPUs, as well as for integrated graphics present on the Intel Core Ultra series processors.

If you are aware, the addition of Forza Horizon 6 is crucial as the game is scheduled to launch in a few days. The game is set in Japan and is one of the major instalments in the Forza series by Xbox Game Studios. Subnautica 2 launches today, and the LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight will launch on May 22, and their addition to Intel's database will introduce optimizations on the Intel Arc hardware.

Apart from the addition of new games, Intel has fixed many known issues and bugs in games. The fixed issues include Battlefield 6 bugs that are related to intermittent corruptions on certain maps during gameplay. Intel has also acknowledged various issues on the Arc B and A series graphics cards, including those that are present on the Core Ultra series processors. These are mostly game-related bugs, which the company is working on and should resolve soon in the upcoming driver updates.

The new Arc graphics driver also adds Performance Metrics Overlay, which allows various customization options such as game/app detection, target GPU, display selection, and appearance tweaks. Users can now also report an issue or a bug using the "Report an Issue/Bug" button available in the Settings -> Support page.

News Source: Intel

About the author: Sarfraz Khan is a hardware reporter with a focus on PC components and the builder community. With years of experience writing about PC hardware and laptops, his work has been featured on several reputable technology publications. Sarfraz's hands-on experience is demonstrated through his first-person accounts of using and comparing different hardware configurations, providing practical and relatable insights for everyday users. His technical analysis is respected by peers in the enthusiast community and has been cited by specialized hardware sites such as Germany's Igor's Lab.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/intel-rolls-out-arc-driver-8801-adding-support-for-forza-horizon-6/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Arc Raiders will get major updates every six months going forwards, with the next one set to roll out the shooter's largest map yet
item.content:encoded:
Devs Embark aim for the extra time to lead to "more transformative updates"

Arc Raiders is switching up its update cadence, with developers Embark having decided that dropping a major set of additions every six months is better for the shooter's long-term health than sticking to a monthly update grind. The first update set to be released under this new modus operandi's also been revealed: it's called Frozen Trail and will include the game's biggest map to date.

"When ARC Raiders launched, we planned to offer monthly updates," Embark executive producer Aleksander Grøndal explained in a blog post outlining the cadence switch. "The intention was to keep you all engaged, to ensure the game always felt fresh, and to give you reasons to keep braving topside. But once the game was in players' hands, we saw that the kind of long-term experience we want to create for ARC Raiders requires more transformative updates.

"Over time, we've found that the pressure of a monthly cycle limits how impactful these updates can be. You feel it, and we feel it too. Running at that pace isn't sustainable, or compatible with the bigger ambitions we have for this game."

These bi-annual updates will aim to be "larger in scale, more impactful, [and] with the goal to genuinely change how you play the game". Grøndal also made clear that smaller live updates will carry on arriving in the gaps between them, alongside "balance fixes, bug fixes, store updates, and player events".

The first major update set to drop following this shift is called Frozen Trail and has been pencilled in for October. It'll include "a new landscape in the Rust Belt with layered design and new mysteries to uncover in the largest map in the game", plus the likes of new enemies, new goals for folks who've hit a progression ceiling, and an improved skill tree. Embark also promise it'll give folks a chance to start investigating what ARC are and where they come from.

Grøndal appeared to paint a bit more of a picture in terms of how that last bit will fit into Arc Raiders' wider narrative plans a bit further on in the post. "Right now, Raiders are facing an escalating ARC threat, and the society in Speranza may feel helpless against it," he wrote. "But Raiders aren't built to stay on the back foot: the goal has always been bigger than survival – it's about one day reclaiming the surface. Frozen Trail will start to progress this goal and expose elements of this challenge, but it is really just the beginning. We aim to constantly shift the paradigm that Raiders operate under so you feel the effects of your actions and perseverance against ARC."

Finally, next week will see the introduction of a trader aimed at offering late-game players a better way to offload unwanted items. He unlocks at level 25 and has rare items, cosmetics, and handy perks to offer in exchange for your unwanted bric-a-brac, as well as errands to fetch specific rare items you can trade in. A "broader set of improvements" is in the works longer-term, seemingly with the hope of convincing more players to head off on progress-resetting expeditions.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/arc-raiders-will-get-major-updates-every-six-months-going-forwards-with-the-next-one-set-to-roll-out-the-shooters-largest-map-yet
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Ubisoft Buries $500,000 in Real Caribbean Treasure for Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced, but Solving It Could Take Five Years
item.content:encoded:
Ubisoft has partnered with French startup Unsolved Hunts to launch Gold & Crystal, a real-life worldwide promotional treasure hunt for Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced that will award $500,000 to the enterprising adventurer who finds the buried treasure.

The $500,000 treasure consists of a crystal skull (a replica of the in-game artifact, made by French lapidary artisan Hervé Obligi) plus a chest full of gold coins minted with the AC Black Flag Resynced logo, produced by AuCOFFRE, a precious metals trading company. The gold coins are worth $350,000, while the crystal skull with an opal is worth $150,000. 

All 15 puzzles can be solved from anywhere in the world with no prior knowledge of the game, making this accessible to non-AC players too. Only the final step, actually digging up the chest, will require traveling to the Caribbean.

Do note that the hunt is "designed to last two to five years", so it won't be a quick thing. Moreover, to participate, you will actually need to pay. There are 7 packs in total, with pricing starting at $35:

Gold & Crystal officially begins on November 9, four months after the launch of Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced, which is slated to debut on July 9.

About the author: With over two decades of experience in gaming journalism, Alessio Palumbo has led the gaming vertical at Wccftech since August 2015. He started working at a young age for Italian websites like Everyeye.it, Gamestar.it, Nextgame.it, and Multiplayer.it before kickstarting the indie English-language publication Worlds Factory as its founder and Editor in Chief. In the last decade, he has coordinated the overall output of Wccftech's gaming section, managed PR relations, assigned reviews, produced daily news coverage, edited gaming content as needed, and delivered game reviews. Arguably, his trademark content is the long series of exclusive developer interviews that have been cited by Wikipedia and by the biggest news media and gaming publications. His passion for technology also makes him knowledgeable when it comes to gaming hardware and tech. His favorite genres include RPGs, MMORPGs, and action/adventure games.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/assassins-creed-black-flag-resynced-real-treasure-hunt-500000/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Fortnite Zero Build Patch Notes: All Zero Build Changes, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Fortnite has gone through several periods of evolution over the news, but a major patch on May 14 is bringing wholesale changes to Zero Build.

The Epic Games mode has become a popular approach in Fortnite for players who don’t want to deal with the complexities of the build mechanics, and the May 14 update is an experimental one, making significant changes to Zero Build.

If you want to see details of all the Zero Build changes in Fortnite, Insider Gaming has you covered.

All Fortnite Zero Build Changes

Fortnite has seen some major adjustments made to Zero Build for the final weeks of Season 2, with the experiment set to impact the future of the game.

Landing alongside the huge Overwatch collab, new items have been added to the loot pool, providing more defensive and utility items in rotation. Additionally, there are new Zero Build exclusive items, Improved Smokle Grenades and Brick-a-Bunkers.

There are also no more fall damage eliminations, as lethal fall damage has been removed. Now, a would-be fatal fall triggers a ‘Splat’ animation, dropping your health to 1 HP and removing all shields and overshield.

  • More Insider Gaming: When Will Fortnite Servers Be Back Up?

All of the Fortnite Zero Build patch notes are below, courtesy of Epic Games:

  • New ‘Pocket Items’ have replaced the old Build button. Pocket Items are unlimited-use utility items with cooldowns; you start every match with one equipped and can swap at Vending Machines around the map. Shockwave Grenades, Med Mist Grenades, and Shield Bubbles are the three Pocket Items available at launch.
  • All players now start matches with a Pistol, a Small Shield Potion, and their Pocket Item.
  • You can now rush to the ground from the Battle Bus. Instead of auto-deploying your Glider near the ground, you can manually cut it and free-fall the last stretch. Be careful!
  • Glider Redeploy returns from elevated positions for cleaner rotations across the map.
  • Holding your Pickaxe grants unlimited Stamina so you can cover ground without burning out.
  • Eliminations now restore your Overshield, rewarding aggressive play.
  • Environmental structures now take significantly less damage from weapons. A boulder, a tree, a random shed will all now hold up as much better cover.
  • Reboot Van timers have been reduced so you can get the squad back in faster.

Are The Zero Build Changes Permanent?

No, the changes made on May 15 in Fortnite’s Zero Build are not permanent, instead serving as an ‘experiment’ for the future of the mode.

Zero Build is themed around “pure gunplay”, and Epic Games wants to “push that identity further,” with the experiment bringing in significant changes for the remainder of Season 2.

  • More Insider Gaming: Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 3 Start Date

Player feedback will shape how Zero Build will be adjusted next season and beyond, and for other Battle Royale modes, showing that Epic Games is ready for a full identity change of the mode.

More details will be shared in future and it remains to be seen how these wholesale changes impact the mode.

What do you think of the Zero Build overhaul in Fortnite? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Fortnite, take a look at all the Rebirth requirements in Droid Tycoon, and check out the full roadmap for Star Wars content in May.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/fortnite-zero-build-changes-patch-notes/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: More Rumours About The Zelda Movie Cast Surface Online
item.content:encoded:
Here we go again

We've already got some surprise news about the live-action Zelda movie today, with Nintendo confirming the release date has shifted from May to April 2027.

Adding to this are some new rumours. According to some casting websites and photos circulating on social media, there'll be...potential spoiler alert...Goron and Kokiri in the upcoming film.

There are reportedly some names attached as well, and the Kokiri girl appears to be based on Link's Nintendo 64 adventure Ocarina of Time, as highlighted by GoNintendo:

"First up, we’ve learned that Albert Latailakepa will be playing a Goron, but we don’t know if he’s tackling a specific Goron from throughout the franchise or just a generic member of the race. We’ve also discovered that a young girl named Evelyn Towersey will be playing Fado in the film. Fado is a Kokiri girl from Ocarina of Time, and she’s the one who teaches the player how to look around in first person."

To top it off, there's also supposedly a photo of a dog on the Zelda production set doing the rounds, and it looks very familiar to a canine featured in Zelda: Breath of the Wild. As many have pointed out, the dog also has an identification badge with the film's rumoured codename "Umami" on it.

This same "Umami" title was previously spotted on a clipboard for the movie. The actors mentioned above also apparently had this name listed on their profiles under their film credentials, but this information appears to have now been removed from the casting websites.

Of course, casting leaks aren't always the most accurate, so Zelda fans may just have to wait until the film is out, or Nintendo officially shares a trailer or more details about its new live-action movie.

What are your thoughts about the latest casting rumours? What extended cast are you expecting to pop up in this live-action movie? Share your thoughts in the comments.

[source gonintendo.com]

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply


https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/05/more-rumours-about-the-zelda-movie-cast-surface-online
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: GTA 6 Pre-Order Date Has Allegedly Leaked Via Best Buy
item.content:encoded:
GTA 6 pre-orders are supposedly set to go live next week, starting May 18, 2026, as a Best Buy email has allegedly leaked when you can place your pre-order. Insider Gaming is currently working on verifying the email, but it does appear to have been received by plenty of people online.

Sent out to affiliates, the email reveals the campaign is for none other than the highly anticipated Grand Theft Auto 6, with the pre-orders set to begin on May 18, five months before the game’s November 19 release date. If pre-orders were to go live, it could very well rule out fears of another delay.

Until we get a hold of the email for ourselves, check out this post by GTA6Alerts on X, who has shared a video of the email, as well as live screenshots of others corroborating the news.

GTA 6 Trailer 3 Could Be Around The Corner

The pre-order news has once again got fans online speculating that the highly rumored Trailer 3 could be right around the corner, with many suspecting that the trailer will close with the official pre-order announcement.

The anticipation surrounding GTA 6 has only continued to grow in recent months. It has now been over a year since Rockstar last showed substantial footage of the game, while Trailer 2 has already surpassed 160 million views on YouTube alone.

With excitement at an all-time high and fans desperate for fresh details, the rumored May 18 pre-order date has many believing Rockstar could finally be preparing to pull back the curtain once again.

Will you be pre-ordering GTA 6, whenever it does go live? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, read about the major changes made to ARC Raiders’ update schedule, as well as Crimson Desert DLC being considered by Pearl Abyss.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/gta-6-pre-order-date-has-allegedly-leaked-via-best-buy/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Today’s Contexto: Hints & Answer For May 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Contexto is a word association game that gives you a new word to guess every single day. With every attempt, the AI algorithm determines how close your guess is to the daily answer based on thematic and contextual similarity.

Sometimes, though, no matter how experienced you are, the word you’re looking for can be incredibly vague and hard to deduce. Luckily for you, this guide is full of hints to give you a helping hand, but make sure you give today’s Contexto a go if you haven’t already!

How To Play Contexto

Contexto is incredibly simple to play, but difficult to master. All you have to do is type words into the search bar, and the AI algorithm will tell you how close your word is to the answer.

You’ll know how close your guess is based on the colour of the bar and the number next to it.

  • Red includes guesses with the number 1,501 and above, meaning you’re way off from the secret word.
  • Orange includes guesses between 301 and 1,500, meaning you’re getting closer, but still some ways off.
  • Green includes guesses between 1 and 300, meaning you’re on the right track.

Best Contexto Tips & Tricks

  • Start off using broad terms, then narrow down once you’ve got under the 1,000 mark.
  • Try using Nouns first, and dip into verbs or adjectives once you’ve discovered the context.
  • Experiment with random words at times, especially as different forms of a root word can rank differently, such as work and worker.
  • Guess away, as unlike other word games like Wordle, you aren’t limited to a strict number of attempts.

Best Starting Words For Contexto

Here are some great starting words to get you going with Contexto:

  • Thing
  • Person
  • Place
  • Animal
  • Work
  • Nature
  • Game
  • Concept
  • Idea
  • Food

Contexto Hints For Today, May 14, 2026

Without giving away today’s Contexto answer, here are a few helpful hints to get you thinking in the right direction:

  • Hint 1: Good
  • Hint 2: Okay
  • Hint 3: Alright

If they don’t help, then here are the nine closest words, going from tenth to second:

  • Additional
  • Even
  • One
  • Pay
  • Penalty
  • Fee
  • Charge
  • Citation
  • Ticket

Today’s Contexto Answer For May 14, 2026

If you’ve tried everything and still can’t figure out today’s Contexto answer, then you can find it down below:

Answer: Fine

Fine often means good or good enough, though it can also refer to a financial punishment.

When Is The Next Contexto Game?

Contexto is updated daily at 12 AM or Midnight in your respective time zone, meaning the next game will start on May 15.

The Contexto schedule is very consistent, with a new game releasing at the same time every day.

How did you find today’s Contexto? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/todays-contexto-hints-answer-for-may-14-2026/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Best Buy appear to have leaked when GTA 6 pre-orders will start, and it’s soon
item.content:encoded:
Multiple Best Buy affiliates have reported getting an email from the company stating that pre-orders for GTA 6  very soon, in a matter of days in fact. This does appear to be a real leak as there is no connection between the multiple sources other than they are all Best Buy affiliates.

According to the notification they received, GTA 6 pre-orders will start on May 18th, with the pre-order affiliate campaign running until May 21st.  Quite why GTA 6 needs affiliates to promote the game is a little puzzling; it’s going to be the biggest-selling game of this year and many years to come.

Presumably, there will also be a new trailer dropping on May 18th to kick off the pre-orders.

The last official news about the game came in November 2025 when Rockstar has announced that Grand Theft Auto 6 has been delayed again, and would now be coming out on 19th November 2026.

“We are sorry for adding additional time to what we realize has been a long wait,” the studio writes, “but these extra months will allow us to finish the game with the level of polish you have come to expect and deserve.

“We want to thank you again for your patience and support. While the wait is a little longer, we are incredibly excited for players to experience the sprawling state of Leonida and a return to modern day Vice City.”

GTA 6 had previously been set for launch on 26th May 2026, and while this was the first firm date that we had for launch from Take Two and Rockstar, it had come after the better part of a year of Take Two execs assuring that the game was going to launch in 2025.

The last we saw of GTA 6 came in May with the release of the game’s second trailer:

Grand Theft Auto VI will take us all back to Vice City for Rockstar’s latest crime story, featuring Lucia, the series’ first female protagonist, who’s coming fresh out of prison, and Jason, who works with the local drugrunners after quitting the army.

Source: Reddit



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/05/14/best-buy-appear-to-have-leaked-when-gta-6-pre-orders-will-start-and-its-soon/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Blue Fury brings back 16-bit helicopter combat action
item.content:encoded:
Persons of a certain age will instantly get the warm fuzzies looking at Blue Fury, a new 16-bit-style helicopter game being created by a one-man development team, namely a chap called Tim Wheatley.

It actually reminds me a lot of the classic Amiga game Virus, but without the awful jet controls, but there were quite a few games that played similarly.

“Each mission drops you over a procedurally generated island – no two maps are the same,” says Tim. “Your objectives are straightforward, but execution is anything but. Destroy enemy tanks, neutralize ground forces and locate friendly soldiers stranded behind enemy lines. Get low, drop a rescue line, and bring them home. All while watching your fuel gauge, managing your ammunition, and keeping one eye on the missile launch countdown timer. When that missile launches, the mission is over.”

The helicopter is armed with a mini-gun as well as rockets but these are unguided so you will need to lock on to your targets manually. The maps feature different biomes and times of year, and there will be a 30-mission campaign along with quick-play options with randomised settings, such as the number of enemies, that you can tweak yourself.

Time for those ever-so-important Key Features!

  • Featuring Helicopter combat inspired by 16-bit classics
  • Procedurally generated island terrain across four biomes
  • Quick mission mode with randomized terrain and customizable enemy loadout
  • Thirty-mission campaign with authored briefings and fixed maps
  • Rescue mechanics with rope and climb animations
  • Missile Silo countdown with multi-stage destruction sequences
  • Enemy helicopters, tanks, AA launchers, gunboats, and jet interceptors
  • Minigun, rockets, and air-to-air missiles
  • Colorblind-friendly minimap option
  • Benchmark and demo mode
  • Scoring system with high score tracking

The game will be released later this month and you can head over to the Blue Fury Steam page for more information and to wishlist.

Source: Steam / Press Release



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/05/14/blue-fury-brings-back-16-bit-helicopter-combat-action/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: GTA 6 Pre-Orders Reportedly Opening This Monday, May 18, According to Leaked Best Buy Email
item.content:encoded:
Rockstar Games might be very close to opening the GTA 6 pre-order campaign. The rumor comes from multiple gamers who, as reported by YouTuber Frogboyx1Gaming, received a Best Buy affiliate email referencing a "GTA 6 Pre-Order (Physical Game)" promotional window running May 18–21, 2026, with a 5% affiliate commission offer. To clarify, this is the promotional window for the aforementioned commission for Best Buy affiliates, not the GTA 6 pre-order period, which will predictably continue until the launch date of November 19.

The email looks like an internal affiliate marketing slip, similar to how retailers have accidentally revealed release dates in the past. Other users from popular gaming forums like ResetEra, GTA Forums, and Cheap Ass Gamer also confirmed receiving the same email, making it unlikely to be an outright fabrication.

The timing aligns with Take-Two's earnings call, which is already scheduled to take place shortly after market close on Thursday, May 21. This schedule actually reinforces the "leak" if we imagine the following strategy from the publisher:

  • May 18 (Monday) — Pre-orders go live, affiliate window opens, media cycle explodes
  • May 19–20 — Pre-order numbers accumulate over the news cycle
  • May 21 (Thursday) — Take-Two CEO Strauss Zelnick opens the earnings call by announcing that GTA 6 has broken every pre-order record in history in four days (extremely likely)

The earnings call becomes a victory lap rather than just a guidance update, the stock gets a double catalyst (pre-order momentum plus earnings), and the GTA 6 hype machine runs at full steam heading into the November launch window.

Just a couple of weeks ago, Zelnick dismissed rumors of a third delay with a casual mention that many gamers would be calling in sick on November 19. A few days later, we learned from verified Glassdoor reviews that Rockstar was working at a hard crunch to meet the game's launch date.

If the leak turns out to be true, we're mere days away from learning the truth behind the pricing of Grand Theft Auto 6. Wedbush Securities analyst Michael Pachter famously scared gamers worldwide by suggesting Rockstar and Take-Two could get away with asking $100, but since then, several analysts have argued the opposite. Even Bank of America would like it to cost $80.

About the author: With over two decades of experience in gaming journalism, Alessio Palumbo has led the gaming vertical at Wccftech since August 2015. He started working at a young age for Italian websites like Everyeye.it, Gamestar.it, Nextgame.it, and Multiplayer.it before kickstarting the indie English-language publication Worlds Factory as its founder and Editor in Chief. In the last decade, he has coordinated the overall output of Wccftech's gaming section, managed PR relations, assigned reviews, produced daily news coverage, edited gaming content as needed, and delivered game reviews. Arguably, his trademark content is the long series of exclusive developer interviews that have been cited by Wikipedia and by the biggest news media and gaming publications. His passion for technology also makes him knowledgeable when it comes to gaming hardware and tech. His favorite genres include RPGs, MMORPGs, and action/adventure games.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/gta-6-pre-orders-may-18-best-buy-leak/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Battlefield 6's New Map Fixes One of the Game's Biggest Issues
item.content:encoded:
Battlefield 6 has received a new map and it fixes one of the biggest issues with the game: a lack of airspace. Thankfully, the team behind Battlefield 6 have addressed this with its latest map, Railway to Golmud. The map is a remake of a Battlefield 4 classic and is naturally already a massive map, but even more so for pilots. The out-of-bounds area for air vehicle stretches far beyond the play area for infantry and ground vehicles, meaning you could theoretically have dogfights without having to worry about anti-air vehicles bothering you.

A core tenet of the Battlefield franchise is its emphasis on all-out warfare. Players can utilize a wide range of vehicles to battle the other team and conquer objectives, including air vehicles, which play a major role on the battlefield. However, the big issue with Battlefield 6 is that the airspace is too small in most maps. Eventually, you hit an out-of-bounds barrier, forcing you to turn back around. Not only does this give you very little room to battle other air vehicles, but can also make it difficult to escape and catch your breath when being locked on to by anti-air weaponry.

While this doesn't fix the issues with every other map, there is hope that those could be improved later in the year. EA has already committed to doing reworks for New Sobek City and Blackwell Fields, two maps that have egregious air space. Both maps are pretty small, so you are constantly being locked on to with little to no room to break the lock-ons. It's expected these reworks will address this issue, but only time will tell how much of a difference it will make.

Battlefield 6 will also be addressing another big issue later in the year. Fans have been underwhelmed with the amount of content the game has been putting out, noting that it's not coming quickly enough and the updates are too small. Season 3 just launched this week and two more seasonal updates are expected this year, with Season 5 aiming to have three new maps instead of just two, rectifying a problem with the live-service side of the game.

Cade Onder is a freelancer for IGN's news team. He covers all things entertainment, including gaming, film, and more. You can find him on Twitter @Cade_Onder.

Recommends

In This Article



https://www.ign.com/articles/battlefield-6s-new-map-fixes-one-of-the-games-biggest-issues
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Arc Raiders Will Only Get Two Major Updates a Year Going Forward and Fans Aren't Happy
item.content:encoded:
Arc Raiders has announced that it will only have two big content updates every year, much to the dismay of players. In a new blog post, Embark Studios revealed that it will release the next big Arc Raiders update, Frozen Trail, in October. This will not only add new locations to explore, but also new enemies, new expansions to the story and lore, and updates to the game's progression systems. It's clear that Embark's ambition is to deliver something expansive and deep in one burst as opposed to drip feeding content throughout the year.

The studio confirmed that it will be on a bi-annual schedule for future content updates.

"Going forward, we've made the decision to release major updates twice a year – larger in scale, more impactful, with the goal to genuinely change how you play the game," reads the blog post. "Don’t worry, a dedicated live service team will continue running ARC Raiders day-to-day: regular live updates, plus balance fixes, bug fixes, store updates, and player events aren't going anywhere.

Arc Raiders Riven Tides Update Screenshots

"This additional development space also allows us to invest more deeply in the health of ARC Raiders, from progression and economy balancing to fair play and anti-cheat efforts. We know these are foundational to the experience, especially in a game where every encounter and extraction matters."

Although the thought process is understandable, some fans think this is the wrong idea. Given how quickly attention can shift to other games or media, some feel that Arc Raiders won't be able to sustain its player base's attention with infrequent updates.

"'Fortnite killer' they said, they really think a live service game can survive with only 2 big updates per year," said one user. "They had a massive hit, the only consistent complaint is lack of follow on content and their response is to basically drop it?" asked another user.

Live service games are all battling for the attention of players and it's extremely difficult. Fortnite had regular updates that not only add new content, but can even radically overhaul the gameplay with new mechanics and abilities. That frequency of change has ensured Fortnite has stayed relevant for almost a decade. Other games, like Battlefield 6, have also been criticized for lackluster and infrequent content updates, but things may be turning around for that game as Season 5 plans to add three maps instead of the usual two.

Arc Raiders still has a strong player base backing it, but the numbers aren't as high as they were when the game launched. When the game launched on Steam, the game consistently had several hundred thousand concurrent players or more. This number has fallen to hover at around 100k or less since the start of spring. It'll be interesting to see how these numbers change with longer gaps between big content drops or if these major updates can meet expectations.

Still, Arc Raiders will continue to get smaller updates to improve the game in-between the bi-annual content drops. Next week, Arc Raiders will get a brand new trader that offers weekly rotating unique and rare rewards. The trader will be available starting at level 25.

Cade Onder is a freelancer for IGN's news team. He covers all things entertainment, including gaming, film, and more. You can find him on Twitter @Cade_Onder.

Recommends

In This Article



https://www.ign.com/articles/arc-raiders-will-only-get-two-major-updates-a-year-going-forward-and-fans-arent-happy
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: IRA coming to Switch 2 this summer
item.content:encoded:
• Switch 2

Roguelike action shoot 'em up currently available for PC.

Publishers Nicalis and Pikii, and developer ABShot will release roguelike action shoot ’em up IRA for Switch 2 via Nintendo eShop this summer, the companies announced.

IRA first launched in Early Access for PC via Steam on August 31, 2021, followed by a full release via Steam, Epic Games Store, and GOG on October 3, 2023.

Here is an overview of the game, via Nicalis:

In IRA, players assume the role of a young girl named Yeon who bravely battles the forces of Chaos by channeling the power of an ancient god and their apostles. Your overarching task is to locate the scattered pieces of the powerful Ira crystal—rebuilding it is the only way to defeat Chaos and restore peace. You attack by firing arrows from any of 150+ different bows, and you can earn many different kinds of power-ups, with both active and passive effects.

Developed by Korean studio ABShot with Nicalis, IRA is a unique anime-inspired shooter with procedurally generated levels and random weapon drops, ensuring a different experience each time it’s played. A key to the game’s appeal are the hypnotic “bullet hell” patterns of the enemies’ (and bosses’) attacks; their projectiles vary greatly in movement type, frequency, visual appearance, speed, size, effects and destructive power. To survive, you must quickly assess their intricate weblike geometry, seek out the short-lived gaps in between and return fire before the bullets overwhelm you.

Watch a new trailer below.

Switch 2 Announce Teaser Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/05/ira-coming-to-switch-2-this-summer
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Trails in the Sky 2nd Chapter details more characters, ‘Brave Rush’ and ‘Enforcer Burst’ systems
item.content:encoded:
Falcom has released new information and screenshots for Trails in the Sky 2nd Chapter introducing characters Campanella, Bleublanc, Walter, Luciola, Josette Capua, Kyle Capua, and Don Capua, as well as the “Brave Rush” and “Enforcer Burst” systems.

Get the details below.

■ Characters

Campanella (voiced by Zach Aguilar in English, Kanae Itou in Japanese)

  • Age: ???

An enigmatic Enforcer of Ouroboros. He has the face of a teenage boy, but his true age is unknown.

Bestowed with the special designation of Enforcer No. 0, Campanella has been dispatched by the Society to simply observe the execution of Weissmann the Faceless’ grand plan.

An elusive figure, Campanella comes and goes in bursts of illusory flame, leaving only cryptic and mocking remarks in his wake.

Phantom Thief Bleublanc (voiced by Kirk Thornton in English, Kishou Taniyama in Japanese)

  • Age: 29

Enforcer No. X, the Phantom Thief.

Conducting nefarious schemes under the alias “Phantom Thief B,” Bleublanc has orchestrated chaos in cities throughout Zemuria. He has joined the Society in pursuit of his aesthetic philosophy: “Beauty is born from dignity—from outshining everyone.”

Bleublanc combines flamboyant theatrics with crafty techniques to bewilder and outmaneuver his foes.

Walter, the Direwolf (voiced by Keith Silverstein in English, Takahiro Yoshimizu in Japanese)

  • Age: 31

Enforcer No. VIII, known as the Direwolf.

Walter is a martial artist who has taken techniques from various martial arts schools across the continent to create his deadly “Murderer’s Fist,” perfectly honed to take human lives.

Fierce and cold-blooded, Walter shows no mercy in combat, even against women and children.

Like the bracer Zin, it seems he has ties to Taito, one of the three great Eastern martial arts schools…

Luciola, the Bewitching Bell (voiced by Natalie Van Sistine in English, Ryouka Yuzuki in Japanese)

  • Age: 27

Enforcer No. VI of Ouroboros. A beguiling woman clad in alluring garb, armed with a pair of folding fans.

Known as the “Bewitching Bell,” Luciola uses her signature bell to conjure illusions and wields her fans to perform attacks as elegant as they are lethal.

Unlike the other Enforcers, she seems to have a deeply personal reason for coming to Liberl…

Josette Capua (voiced by Risa Mei in English, Anna Nagase in Japanese)

  • Age: 16

The youngest of the Capua siblings and the only girl amongst the Sky Pirates, Josette is a skillful wielder of both arts and her orbal gun.

Though she has a fiery attitude on the surface, Josette is actually a responsible figure who handles all the sky pirate gang’s day-to-day needs. The other pirates, aside from her brothers, respectfully refer to her as “Miss.”

She and Estelle never see eye to eye, and their interactions inevitably devolve into childish bickering when their paths cross.

Kyle Capua (voiced by Kieran Regan in English, Atsushi Tamaru in Japanese)

  • Age: 23

The middle child of the Capua siblings and the pilot of their airship.

Kyle’s innate piloting talent lets him maneuver the mobile Bobcat with ease, and his keen intellect often makes him the crew’s primary strategist.

While he tries to respect Josette’s wishes, he seems to harbor misgivings deep down about his own younger sister risking her life as part of their pirate gang.

Don Capua (voiced by Harry Wood in English, Teruyuki Tanzawa in Japanese)

  • Age: 30

The eldest Capua sibling and big boss of their crew.

Though he was manipulated into committing malicious crimes during the events of 1st Chapter, Don’s true personality is that of a courageous, straightforward man devoted to his family and friends.

After he and his crew were imprisoned, they took advantage of the confusion in Liberl in the wake of the coup attempt, breaking out of jail and disappearing.

■ System

Brave Rush

Trails in the Sky 2nd Chapter introduces new, wide-range, high-damage Brave Rush attacks! Use two Brave Gauge points in Quick Battle to pair your active character with a party member and unleash a devastating combo strike!

The Brave Rush mechanic joins the lineup of normal attacks, jump attacks, dodges, and character-specific Crafts to further expand your tactical options in battle!

Enforcer Boost

An advanced version of Rage Boost that can only be used by the Enforcers of Ouroboros.

When activated, a character-specific animation plays and the appearance of the battlefield changes.

Since the boost state lasts longer than a standard Rage Boost, the battle difficulty increases dramatically!

—About Rage Boost

Some powerful enemies and bosses can enter a Rage Boost state. While active, they gain unavoidable attacks, halved delay, and can even steal AT Bonuses. Since various other stats are also buffed, the tide of battle can be turned against you in an instant…!



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/05/trails-in-the-sky-2nd-chapter-details-more-characters-brave-rush-and-enforcer-burst-systems
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Capcom Says It Will Continue To Nurture Its "Leading Brands" With Sequels, Remakes And Ports
item.content:encoded:
New IP is also part of the plan

Capcom has already had an outstanding run this year, and if you're wondering what its plans are looking ahead, it's effectively more of the same.

As we briefly touched on in our previous story, the presentation slides from the company's latest financial report reveal how it intends to nurture its leading brands to be the "next engine of growth" with the release of "sequels, remakes, ports" and by continuing to add "new IP" to the mix.

Some of the series listed include Mega Man (with his new game Dual Override due out next year), Devil May Cry and Onimusha.

As you can see in the slide above, the company's business model is all about IP expansion and development through various IP and sales strategies. It's even acknowledged the Monster Hunter series alongside the video game legends Ryu and Leon.

So far this year, Capcom has released Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection, Resident Evil Requiem, Mega Man Star Force: Legacy Collection and most recently Pragmata, with an update earlier this month revealing the game sold over two million copies worldwide in just 16 days. The new Resident Evil outing also got a free update last week.

What would you like to see from Capcom next? Are there any series you would like to see it revive? Have you played any of its new games in 2026? Let us know in the comments.

[source capcom.co.jp]

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply


https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/05/capcom-says-it-will-continue-to-nurture-its-leading-brands-with-sequels-remakes-and-ports
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Drecom establishes indie game studio Drecom Creators Studio
item.content:encoded:
Exhibiting at BitSummit PUNCH from May 22 to 24.

Drecom has announced the establishment of Drecom Creators Studio, an “indie game studio dedicated to supporting game creators, discovering and nurturing talent, and bringing their works to the world.”

The newly founded studio will exhibit at BitSummit PUNCH, which will run from May 22 to 24 at Miyako Messe in Kyoto, Japan, where it will have a demo area set up for titles it is supporting. Details about the playable demo titles will be announced in the leadup to the exhibition.

Here is an overview of the studio, via Drecom:

The Vision of Drecom Creators Studio

Drecom Creators Studio is an indie game studio dedicated to discovering the potential of works created by game creators and providing comprehensive support, from development through to long-term intellectual property expansion. We cater to a wide range of projects, from solo creators to small teams, and aim to ensure that these works continue to be loved across generations and borders while preserving the creators’ artistic vision and the essence of their work.

Through comprehensive support, including the sharing of development expertise, financial assistance, marketing support, and integrated media campaigns. We create an environment where creators can focus on their work, unlock the full potential of their creations, and together bring them to the world.

Background to the Establishment of Drecom Creators Studio

In recent years, Drecom has been working to bring excitement to a wider audience through the provision of IP-based entertainment content. By supporting game creators, discovering and nurturing their works, and bringing them to the world, we aim to contribute to the growth of the game industry including its creators, and to the advancement of Japanese entertainment.

Message From Drecom Creators Studio

Drecom is involved in media mix projects, including novelizations, comic adaptations, anime adaptations, and merchandise, that go beyond game development, operation, and distribution. One notable example of this is our commitment to the Wizardry series, which has continued to captivate audiences worldwide for over 40 years. We have created Blade & Bastard from this great intellectual property and have realized a comprehensive media mix spanning novels, comics, anime adaptations, and merchandise.

In addition, the 3D dungeon RPG Wizardry Variants Daphne (iOS, Android, Steam), which has been available since October 2024, has surpassed three million downloads worldwide. Even as we speak, it continues to captivate adventurers around the globe.

We at Drecom Creators Studio are currently seeking creators to join us in crafting the stories of the future, drawing on the expertise and passion we have cultivated through our work in intellectual property production.

Detailed information about Drecom Creators Studio and the application for creators are now available on the official website. We look forward to receiving applications from many creators.

Visit Drecom Creators Studio’s official website here.



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/05/drecom-establishes-indie-game-studio-drecom-creators-studio
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Granblue Fantasy: Relink – Endless Ragnarok pre-orders now available
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS4
  • PS5
  • Switch 2

New playable characters Fraux and Fediel revealed.

Pre-orders for Granblue Fantasy: Relink – Endless Ragnarok are now available, publisher Cygames and developer Cygames Osaka announced.

The game is available in the following editions:

  • Granblue Fantasy: Relink – Endless Ragnarok
    • Standard Edition ($59.99 / £49.99 / €59.99 / ¥ 6,930)
      • Granblue Fantasy: Relink
      • Granblue Fantasy: Relink – Endless Ragnarok Upgrade Kit
    • Special Edition ($79.99 / £64.99 / €79.99 / ¥9,900)
      • Granblue Fantasy: Relink
      • Granblue Fantasy: Relink – Endless Ragnarok Upgrade Kit
      • Granblue Fantasy: Relink – Endless Ragnarok Special Pack – Includes special character colors for the captain, Id, and Sandalphon. Also comes with useful items for unlocking Seofon, Tweyen, and Sandalphon.
      • Concept Art Color Pack
        • The Captain (Gran/Djeeta)
        • Id
        • Sandalphon
      • Azurite’s Splendor (x5)
      • Canaan Whitebloom (x3)
      • Supreme Weapon Essence (x6)
      • Granblue Fantasy: Relink Downloadable Content Collection – This is a collection of previously released downloadable content for Granblue Fantasy: Relink. It includes useful items for making upgrades, 5 sets of colors for characters, and three sets of emotes.
        • Ignition Pack
          • Weapon: False Sword of the Apocalypse
          • Glittercrystal (x30)
          • 500 MSP
        • Fortitude Crystal (S) (x50)
        • Color Packs 1 to 5
        • Emote Expansion Sets: Let’s Chat, I Work Out, Grab Bag
  • Granblue Fantasy: Relink – Endless Ragnarok Upgrade Kit
    • Upgrade Kit Standard Edition ($29.99 / £24.99 / €29.99 / ¥3,960)
      • Granblue Fantasy: Relink – Endless Ragnarok Upgrade Kit
    • Upgade Kit Special Edition ($54.99 / £44.99 / €54.99 / ¥ 6,490)
      • Granblue Fantasy: Relink – Endless Ragnarok Upgrade Kit
      • Granblue Fantasy: Relink – Endless Ragnarok Special Pack – Includes special character colors for the captain, Id, and Sandalphon. Also comes with useful items for unlocking Seofon, Tweyen, and Sandalphon.
      • Concept Art Color Pack
        • The Captain (Gran/Djeeta)
        • Id
        • Sandalphon
      • Azurite’s Splendor (x5)
      • Canaan Whitebloom (x3)
      • Supreme Weapon Essence (x6)
      • Granblue Fantasy: Relink Downloadable Content Collection – This is a collection of previously released downloadable content for Granblue Fantasy: Relink. It includes useful items for making upgrades, 5 sets of colors for characters, and three sets of emotes.
        • Ignition Pack
          • Weapon: False Sword of the Apocalypse
          • Glittercrystal (x30)
          • 500 MSP
        • Fortitude Crystal (S) (x50)
        • Color Packs 1 to 5
        • Emote Expansion Sets: Let’s Chat, I Work Out, Grab Bag

Pre-orders for all editions include the “Guts III & Autorevive III Sigil Set,” which contains the “Guts III Sigil” and “Autorevive III Sigil.” Pre-orders on PlayStation 5, PlayStation 4, and PC include immediate access to the base Granblue Fantasy: Relink game and Granblue Fantasy: Relink Downloadable Content Collection where applicable.

A new set of trailers was also released. The “Strength and Darkness” trailer reveals new playable characters Fraux and Fediel, as well as the new foe The World. The “Summons and the Conflux” trailer introduces the  Summons and Conflux systems. Here are the details, via the official website:

New Playable Characters

  • Fraux (voiced by Kelly Baskin in English, Azumi Asakura in Japanese) – Striking Strength. Pactbearer to The Devil, a primal seeking to create a new world order, Fraux is otherwise an ordinary skydweller whose heart, character, and temperament are filled with love and compassion.
  • Fediel (voiced by Savy Des-Etages in English, Asami Tano in Japanese) – Darkness Personified. Fediel is one of the Six Dragons, entities who exist beyond the confines of the natural world. Having dominion over darkness, she influences the very laws of nature.

New Foe

  • The World (voiced by Kellen Goff in English, Atsushi Ono in Japanese) – Inchoate World. One of the Arcarum, The World is the plinth and pillar to which the other Arcarum are tied. Originally created by the Astrals in ancient times, it eventually evolved to a state of self-awareness and self-actualization, developing its own identity.

New Systems

  • Summons – Call forth the power of enemies and allies alike. Summons provide a new dimension to combat with their own unique attack skills and powerful support abilities.
  • Primal Bursts – Summon the might of Proto Bahamut and Excavallion. Once certain battle conditions are fulfilled, activate Skybound Arts with all four members of the party to obliterate your foes with a stunning primal burst.
  • Chaos Quests – Face off against the dread might of the Ragnalia in the most challenging tier of quests yet. Tackling Chaos quests will unfurl a new chapter in the Relink saga and shed light on the fresh chaos consuming Zegagrande
  • The Conflux – Solve puzzles and fight your way through a multitude of battles to arrive at the heart of a dimension replete with singular power. Take advantage of special traits and buffs only available in the Conflux to power up your characters and conquer the awaiting trials. Should you succeed in plumbing its depths, you’ll obtain a wealth of treasure to enhance your characters and weapons and bring out their true power.
  • Cross-Play – Endless Ragnarok features crossplay between Nintendo Switch 2, PlayStation 5, PlayStation 4, and Steam. Nintendo Switch 2 players will also be able to use Local Communication to engage in up to four-player cooperative play!

And here is an overview of the game, via Cygames:

About

Known for its dynamic combat, ensemble of versatile characters, and thrilling online cooperative play, Granblue Fantasy: Relink returns with expanded solo and multiplayer content, bringing endless adventure to skyfarers both new and old.

Key Features

  • Band Together for Exhilarating Battles – Form a party of four and depart for battle in this epic action RPG. Each character sports distinct combat mechanics, allowing for a whole crewful of unique playstyles. Weave these playstyles together using satisfying party-based attacks—like link attacks and chain bursts—to really make combat soar. Even recruits fresh to action gaming can take advantage of Assist Mode to reach the story’s heart-pounding conclusion.
  • Embark on an Endless Variety of Cooperative Quests – A wide range of quests become available as the adventure unfolds. From unique boss fights to horde-survival missions, all quests are playable offline or online with up to four players. Whether it’s casual questing or ultra-challenging end content, there’s something for everyone to enjoy.
  • Major Additions in Endless Ragnarok – From new stories to game modes, Granblue Fantasy: Relink has been refined and revamped for the ultimate action RPG experience.
  • Embark on New Adventures – Although the Astral Lilith’s machinations lay thwarted, winds of change begin to stir within the Zegagrande Skydom… Strange beings called the “ragnalia” herald the end times. Mysterious gateways beckon would-be challengers into a realm of chaos. And even the mighty Beelzebub, with his wings of steel, descends upon Zegagrande.
  • Call in the Big Guns – During their journey, players will unlock the ability to equip and call forth summons, adding a new dimension to battle strategy. Once channeled by Lyria, some summons can be temporarily controlled by the player; but if certain conditions are met, others will make a spectacular entrance alongside a new type of chain burst—the “primal burst.”
  • More to Master, More to Play
    • New cooperative quest tiers and additional bosses.
    • The Conflux, an all-new solo mode where unpredictable challenges and enigmatic powers lurk within its depths.
    • The introduction of master traits to push characters beyond their limits.
    • Summons to expand on tactical options.
    • All of these features—and more—ensure that even after the credits roll, Granblue Fantasy: Relink‘s skies remain vast!
  • Play Across Boundaries – Granblue Fantasy: Relink’s thrilling four-player online cooperative combat reaches new heights with cross-play across all platforms!

Granblue Fantasy: Relink – Endless Ragnarok is due out for PlayStation 5, Switch 2, PlayStation 4, and PC via Steam on July 9.

Watch the trailers below.

Strength and Darkness Trailer

English

Japanese

Summons and the Conflux

English

Japanese



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/05/granblue-fantasy-relink-endless-ragnarok-pre-orders-now-available
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: LIMIT ZERO BREAKERS ‘Prologue Test’ set for June 10 to 15
item.content:encoded:
• PC

Sign-ups now available.

Publisher NCSOFT and developer Vic Game Studios will host the “Prologue Test’ for anime-style action RPG LIMIT ZERO BREAKERS from June 10 to 15, the companies announced. It will be available for PC via Steam, iOS, and Android. Sign-ups are available now via the official website.

Here are the details:

Prologue Test Recruitment & Schedule Information

  • Recruitment Period: Through June 2, 2026 at 6:00 AM (PDT)
  • Participants: Selected at random from users who signed up for the Prologue Test
  • Test Period: June 10, 2026 to June 15, 2026
  • Platforms: PC (Steam), Android, iOS​

Prologue Test Sign-up Information

  • You can sign up via the [Prologue Test Sign-up] button on the official website.

*When signing up for mobile platforms (Android/iOS), please use an active email address.
*For Steam sign-ups, please complete your application by clicking the “Request Access” button on the Steam page after being redirected. This will successfully submit your application. (Additional instructions will be provided separately through the official announcement.)

Participant Selection Announcement Information

  • Selected participants will be announced separately after the recruitment period ends. Additional details regarding pre-download and participation instructions will also be shared at that time.

Notes

  • A full FAQ is available here.
  • The LIMIT ZERO BREAKERS Prologue Test will be conducted as a closed test with a limited number of participants. Only selected users will be eligible to participate.
  • All communications and access permissions will be provided based on the email address submitted during the Prologue Test sign-up. Please make sure your email address is entered correctly.
  • Please note that users who are not selected will not receive a separate notification email.
  • Selection notifications will be sent out in stages and may be subject to delays depending on internal circumstances.
  • The Prologue Test build is still under development. As such, server instability and bugs may occur, and maintenance may be conducted during the test period as needed.

Watch a new trailer below.

Prologue Test Trailer

English

Japanese

Korean



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/05/limit-zero-breakers-prologue-test-set-for-june-10-to-15
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Nintendo Expands Switch Online's Virtual Boy Library With Five More Games
item.content:encoded:
Nintendo has today added five Virtual Boy titles to its Switch Online + Expansion Pack 'Nintendo Classics' service.

This includes the maze game Jack Bros. from Atlus, a Space Invaders release from Taito Corporation, Bullet-Proof's title V-Tetris, Hudson Soft's shoot 'em up Vertical Force and Athena's Virtual Bowling - with all titles originally released on the headset platform in 1995.

Here's the official announcement from Nintendo, along with the lineup of games that are now available:

Nintendo: "Five classic Virtual Boy titles are now available to play on Nintendo Switch 2 and Nintendo Switch for Nintend Switch Online + Expansion Pack members!"

Nintendo Classics - Virtual Boy Update (May 2026)

V-Tetris

"In this classic puzzle game released only in Japan for the Virtual Boy™ system in 1995, you'll stack falling Tetriminos (also known as Tetris® blocks) to form lines with no gaps and clear them. This game also contains another mode, Loop Tetris®, where strategic use of the looping field can allow you to clear five lines or more in one go!"

Jack Bros.

"Can you make it back before midnight? The three mischievous Jack Brothers are on a Halloween romp through the human world. They lose track of time in all their merry-making and realize they only have one hour until the portal back to the World of Fairies closes and they're banished forever! Now they must follow Pixie the Fairy down a dangerous shortcut to make it back in time. The brothers will have to collect all of the keys on each dungeon floor to open the exit and jump down to the next floor. It's a race against the clock in this action title originally released for the Virtual Boy™ system in 1995. Solve puzzles, battle powerful enemies, and follow Pixie's lead to make it home before the clock strikes midnight!"

Space Invaders: Virtual Collection

"Experience the worldwide hit arcade game that released only in Japan on the Virtual Boy™ system in 1995. Repel Invaders who encroach from the back of the screen. Play in Original 2D, Virtual 3D, or Challenge mode, where you'll try for the best clear time and score. In the Original 2D and Virtual 3D modes, you can select whether you'd like to play the original SPACE INVADERS or SPACE INVADERS Part II."

Virtual Bowling

"In this title released exclusively in Japan on the Virtual Boy™ system in 1995, experience the fun of bowling in an incredible 3D space. From the pins to the lanes, everything has been replicated in this immersive virtual bowling alley. You can even adjust settings, like ball weight and dominant arm, to take the immersion to the next level!"

Vertical Force

"The planetary-caretaker computer of planet Odin has spontaneously reprogrammed itself and declared war on all of humanity under the name Mittgard. As the United Earth Army fights a losing battle, you and the Ragnarok battle machine you pilot are humanity's only hope. Use Ragnarok's flightline system to move between two different altitudes, and deploy AI Drones that automatically tail and attack enemy fighters in this unique play experience originally released for the Virtual Boy™ system in 1995."

Japan's lineup this week also includes one extra title. It's the 1995 fishing game Virtual Fishing, published by Pack-In-Video.

If you want to try it out, you'll need to have access to a Japanese Nintendo account, and you can then download the Japanese version of the Virtual Boy app from the eShop.

Virtual Fishing (Japan)

"This title is a fishing action game for virtual boys released in 1995. With the unique depth of Virtual Boy, you can enjoy casting in the great outdoors and thrilling battles with fish. There are competitions for each type of fish, and if you win, a time attack mode opens where you compete for the time to catch a set number of fish. In addition, in free play, you can challenge fishing spots where 17 types of fish live, including those that cannot be caught in tournaments."

To play these titles on the service, you'll also need access to the Expansion Pack tier, and to play the way Nintendo intends, you'll require one of its headsets, which can be purchased from the Nintendo Store.

As part of the Virtual Boy library update this week, Nintendo has also released a new wave of Virtual Boy Switch Online icons.

Will you be revisiting any of these games on this subscription service? What other titles would you like to see join the Nintendo Classics library? Let us know in the comments.



https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/05/nintendo-expands-switch-onlines-virtual-boy-library-with-five-more-games

item.title: Virtual Boy – Nintendo Classics adds V-Tetris, Jack Bros., Space Invaders Virtual Collection, Virtual Bowling, and Vertical Force
item.content:encoded:
• Switch
  • Switch 2

Plus Virtual Fishing in Japan.

The Virtual Boy – Nintendo Classics app has added V-Tetris, Jack Bros., Space Invaders Virtual Collection, Virtual Bowling, and Vertical Force, Nintendo announced. In Japan, Virtual Fishing was also added.

Watch a trailer below.

May 2026 Game Updates

English

Japanese

item.title: The Live-Action Zelda Movie Has A New Worldwide Release Date
item.content:encoded:
April is the new May

Breaking news! Nintendo has announced it's changing the release date of The Legend of Zelda live-action movie.

In a new announcement via the mobile app Nintendo Today! and Nintendo's social media page, it's been revealed the film will now arrive "worldwide" in cinemas on 30th April 2027.

Nintendo's Shigeru Miyamoto has issued the following message, noting how the date has been shifted from May to April. Here it is in full:

"This is Miyamoto. I would like to let you know that the worldwide theatrical release date for the live-action film of The Legend of Zelda has been moved up to April 30, 2027, from May 7. The team is working hard to deliver the film to everyone as soon as possible. There’s less than a year to go until release, so thank you for waiting."

The Legend of Zelda movie was announced in November 2023 and was originally scheduled to arrive in theatres in March 2027. It was then delayed "for production reasons" in June last year, shifting its launch back a few months, and it's now been moved up a month to April.

What are your thoughts about the new release date? Do you think it will stick this time? Let us know in the comments.

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply


https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/05/the-live-action-zelda-movie-has-a-new-worldwide-release-date
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: NYT Strands #801 Hints and Answers for Today, May 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #801 for Wednesday, May 13, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters and under do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints For Today, May 13, 2026

The Strands’ answers to the theme of Game #801 are all related to terms that describe one’s readiness to do something or their unmitigated gall.

General Strands Hints For May 13

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: Your doctor might suggest eating more of this if you have constipation issues.
  • Hint 2: This symbol is your life indicator in The Legend of Zelda.
  • Hint 3: When someone is shocked by something that you’ve said or done, they might say you have a lot of this

Use these clues to narrow down your searches and increase your chances of solving Strands #801 for May 13, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for May 13 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • TAKES
  • TAKE
  • MAKES
  • MAKE
  • LUCK

Today’s Strands Answer For Game #801 On May 13, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • GRIT
  • FIBER
  • HEART
  • PLUCK
  • NERVE
  • GUMPTION
  • SPUNK
  • SPANAGRAM: WHATITTAKES

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #801?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on May 14 will be Strands Game #802.

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett serves as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since its launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/nyt-strands-801-hints-and-answers-for-today-may-13-2026/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Nobody Needs To Know What An Analyst Thinks About A Singleplayer Video Game's Sales. Nobody!
item.content:encoded:
Don't burden yourself with information that's not for you

Published: May 13, 2026, 8:23 pm

Last updated: May 13, 2026, 8:27 pm

Just as it's deeply annoying to see people caring about a singleplayer video game's player count, it's maybe even more annoying to see people caring about a singleplayer video game's sales when it’s only been out for two weeks.

An example--I'm not picking on just this report, this is a common subject in games media--is a Eurogamer story from this morning titled "Saros is off to a slower start than Returnal, with some analysts concerned Housemarque 'will struggle to break even'". Eurogamer isn't a trade publication; it's a mainstream website, whose audience is primarily made up of fans and consumers. 

And it's like...OK? I don't know why any normal person should care, or think they should care, about any of this. You, the reader, are someone who plays and maybe sometimes buys video games. Saros is a singleplayer video game which is played without the need of a community, online support or active playerbase. Your only concerns here, then, should be whether you enjoy the game you played or not.

You're not an investor! You don't work at developers Housemarque or PlayStation; why should you possibly care what the game's long-term financial future should be when you, a normal person, have zero knowledge of the company's budget or the leash it's on with Sony?

And why should you care about any of this when the bad vibes being spoken about are coming from an analyst, someone whose job involves talking to publishers and investors, not the general game-playing public.

I wish games websites would stop running stories like this, and I wish normal people would stop thinking they matter. This news isn't for you. Normal people walking around carrying this knowledge with them are being burdened with information that can only do them harm, because reading stuff like this can affect your perceptions of the game, the studio responsible for it and the platform it's played on that actually have no bearing on your singleplayer experience whatsoever.

If you like Housemarque and want to play Saros, play it! And if you like it, great!



https://aftermath.site/saros-ps5-sales/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Subnautica 2 Will Be Available on GeForce Now
item.content:encoded:
There is good news for anyone who subscribes to Nvidia GeForce Now and was hoping to get their hands on Subnautica 2 as quickly as possible. The game will be coming to the platform when it releases in Early Access on Steam, Epic Games Store, and Xbox Game Pass on May 14. The announcement was made by the development team on social media on May 13, just one day before release.

More on Subnautica 2’s Early Access Release

Early Access will be available only to PC and Xbox players, but the game is expected to come to other platforms later. In the past, Subnautica and Subnautica: Below Zero both came to PlayStation 5 and PlayStation 4; the initial early access build for the new game has elected to skip Sony platforms for now.

This new game is crafted as a solo experience. However, this time around, you can play online co-op with up to 3 players. The game has already surpassed 5 million wish lists on Steam, and the developers at Unknown Worlds are excited for players to dive into the Early Access experience.

“Subnautica 2 is an underwater survival adventure game set on an all-new alien world. It is the next chapter in the Subnautica universe, developed by Unknown Worlds. Driven from your home by ongoing conflict, Alterra offers you the chance at a new life. But as the colony ship CICADA shepherds you and your fellow Pioneers to your new home, something goes awry. The ship’s AI insists that your mission should continue. Stranded and faced with near-insurmountable odds, you must do everything in your power to survive. The future of humanity on this world is in your hands,” the game’s synopsis states.

For more on everything you need to know ahead of the Early Access release of Subnautica 2, you can check out everything we know about the Early Access roadmap in this handy guide. Insider Gaming will continue to monitor the game in its Early Access phase and report all updates.

Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/subnautica-2-will-be-available-on-geforce-now/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Capcom Outlines How Its AI Use Will Allow More Time for Creativity
item.content:encoded:
Capcom has admitted that it will continue researching and using generative AI. However, the company has also outlined what they will use the technology for and how it will allow them to spend more time on creative endeavors and the development of new projects.

On May 13, Capcom announced its financial results for the last quarter of the fiscal year ending in 2026. In its report, Capcom disclosed that its research and use of generative AI are part of its ongoing investments in its popular IPs. As with many companies, they also stated that the technology is primarily utilized to enhance efficiency with the development process.

“As it is crucial to maintain and improve its core strengths, consisting of its proprietary advanced technologies and development capabilities, the Company continues to aggressively pursue growth investments aimed at building its development environment,” said Capcom in its earnings report.

“This includes research and development into cutting-edge technologies such as its proprietary game engine, other development investments, and the use of generative AI to enhance the efficiency and productivity of the game development processes. Through these efforts, the company is working to further expand and deepen the penetration of its content and corporate brand worldwide by consistently releasing content to the market and increasing catalog sales, thereby enhancing its brand value.”

Capcom also provided a graphical representation of how the technology will be utilized moving forward. As part of one of the slides in its earnings presentation, Capcom stated, “AI utilized to improve development efficiency and productivity
A tool to streamline routine tasks, freeing up time for creative work.” This statement was accompanied by a graphic noting that the use of the new technology has freed up time previously spent on research, draft generation, user analysis, interactive manuals, error checks, meeting notes, and more.

The topic of AI in game development has become increasingly prevalent. Sony’s president recently stated that he believes AI will power the future of their games. Read more of those comments here. Sony has also stated that they are working with Bandai Namco to learn how to better use generative AI in the future. Read more about that here.

For more from Capcom, check out further details on their record-breaking fiscal year. Insider Gaming will continue to monitor the story and report any updates as more information becomes available.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/capcom-outlines-how-its-ai-use-will-allow-more-time-for-creativity/
